name: bulk-extractor
title: bulk-extractor
description:
Extracts information without parsing filesystem
bulk_extractor is a C++ program that scans a disk image, a file,
or a directory of files and extracts useful information without
parsing the file system or file system structures. The results are
stored in feature files that can be easily inspected, parsed, or
processed with automated tools. bulk_extractor also creates
histograms of features that it finds, as features that are more
common tend to be more important.

commands.name: bulk_extractor
commands.description: Scans a disk image for regular expressions and other content.

arguments.description: Specifies the size of the context window.
arguments.raw: -C  Specifies the size of the context window.

arguments.description: Specifies a regular expression to be used as a search term.
arguments.raw: -f  Specifies a regular expression to be used as a search term.

arguments.description: Specifies the size of the margin in bytes.
arguments.raw: -g  Specifies the size of the margin in bytes.

arguments.description: Have bulk_extractor wait at most NN minutes for scanners to finish after all data have
arguments.raw: -m  Have bulk_extractor wait at most NN minutes for scanners to finish after all data have

arguments.description: Specifies a directory in which to find plugins.
arguments.raw: -P  Specifies a directory in which to find plugins.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use bulk_extractor because Extracts information without parsing filesystem.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use bulk_extractor as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bulk_extractor -o output_dir [options] [ image | -R dir ]` as documented upstream for bulk_extractor.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair bulk_extractor with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `bulk_extractor --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
